name: Surprise Pasta (c) 1992 George Gilder
recipeIngredient: 8 Chicken supremes or 2 large Boneless breasts, cut into 1-inch slices
1 Red bell pepper and 1 Green bell pepper
1/4 cup Flour flavored with Orange juice
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the onion ends off and peel. Slice in half as you would an apple. Place each half, cut side down, and slice 1/4-inch slices. Slice the peppers in half and core, then seed them. With a sharp knife, cut very thin julienned slices of half (or whole if smaller).
2. In a large pot, add cold water. Bring to boil, then add pasta. Stir occasionally to separate pieces. When boiling again, cook for seven minutes, or until tender. Drain and return pasta to the pot (turn off flame). Add 1 tablespoon margarine; stir to coat.
3. Heat a ten-inch Teflon-coated saute pan with half the oil over medium-low heat. Sauté onion until just beginning to brown. Add pepper pieces for about two minutes. Add mushrooms, coating them in oil for approximately 90 seconds. Transfer vegetables to a bowl, leaving about three tablespoons of mixture in the pan.
4. While cooking the vegetables, prepare the chicken by washing and drying the pieces. Place the flavored flour (orange juice) into a small plastic bag; add chicken and shake to coat evenly. In the same pan with remaining oil, saute chicken until lightly browned on both sides but not overcooked. Add thyme and cooked vegetables. Cover and simmer for five minutes until chicken is cooked through yet moist.
5. Divide pasta among plates. Spoon the rich brown sauce over top, then arrange chicken pieces over the pasta. Serve immediately.
